prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, exact four-center integrals, no density fitting; tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet). Cation state: charge +1, multiplicity 2 (doublet). Anion state: charge −1, multiplicity 2 (doublet). Mulliken partial charges used for finite-difference Fukui indices. All three SCF runs converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true. Sum checks: Σf⁺ = 1.0000 ✅, Σf⁻ = 1.0000 ✅. No engine warnings emitted.

Headline result: The most electrophilic site is the carbonyl carbon (atom 2, C) with f⁺ = 0.70839 (reported as the headline value). The most nucleophilic site is the oxygen (atom 1, O) with f⁻ = 0.46742. Dual descriptor: C = +0.59595 (electrophilic), O = +0.00071 (ambiphilic), H = −0.298 (nucleophilic). This is consistent with the known carbonyl chemistry of formaldehyde — the carbon is the primary site for nucleophilic addition. No warnings were reported by the engine. The routine integrity checks confirm correct charge conservation for both f⁺ and f⁻ columns.
